rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MAYO VS. ALICE TSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf To Provide Supplemental Responses To Req.'S For Innovistech Llc.'S Form Interrogatories; Etc And For Monetary Sanctions

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MAYO VS. ALICE TSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defts' Request For Relief From Late Filing Of Defts' Motion To Compel Pltf'S Further Responses To Defts Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions, For Excusable Neglect, Mistake Or Inadvertence, Or In The Alternative, For Leave Of Court To Amend Defts' Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 13, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS ALICE TSE, ESCALADE CAPITAL GROUP, AND INNOVISTECH, LLC'S Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendants' Request For Relief From Late Filing Of Defendants' Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Defendants Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions, For Excusable Neglect, Mistake Or Inadvertence, Or In The Alternative, For Leave Of Court To Amend Defendants' Motion To Compel. MOTION DENIED AS UNTIMELY. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING OF MISTAKE INADVERTENCE SURPRISE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THIS MATTER WILL BE HEARD ON TODAY'S (OCTOBER 13) 9:00A.M. DISCOVERY CALENDAR. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KIM BELLINO VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Comepl Production Of Medical Records In Response To Lawful Subpoenas And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 13, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. AND LENARD RITZMAN'S Motion To Compel Production Of Medical Records In Response To Lawful Subpoenas And For Sanctions. MOTION TO COMPEL GRANTED. BOTH PARTIES MOTIONS FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. BOTH PARTIES ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. WHILE PLAINTIFF COULD HAVE BEEN MORE ACCOMODATING, NO CITED LAW REQUIRES HER TO SIGN A RELEASE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MINOTT WESSINGER AS TRUSTEE OF THE MINOTT VS. EDWARD KAVOUNAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Protective Order And Sanctions In The Amount Of $2450 And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 13, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT ARMOUR & VOKIC ARCHITECTURE'S Motion For A Protective Order And Sanctions In The Amount Of $2450. MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2450 GRANTED. THE DOCUMENTS WERE LABELED "PRIVILEGED" WHICH IS A CLEAR INDICATOR THAT THEY ARE PRIVILEGED FOR PURPOSES OF THIS MOTION. THEIR INADVERTENT DISCLOSURE BY COUNSEL DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A WAIVER OF THE PRIVILEGE. DEFENDANT MADE A GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO MEET AND CONFER. CCP SECTION 2031.060. THE COURT ADOPTS DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID SIEGEL VS. STEPHEN BACHMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 13, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT WILLIAM TIMKEN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATION) PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE DEFENDANTS KNEW THEY WERE FALSE. PLAINTIFF MUST PARTICULARLY IDENTIFY THE ALLEGED FALSE REPRESENTATIONS THAT FORM THE BASIS OF THE 1ST, 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (CONVERSION) PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THE DEFENDANTS' INDIVIDUAL PARTICIPATION. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (INDUCING BREACH OF CONTRACT) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD A THEORY NOT BARRED BY SHOEMAKER V. MYERS (1990) 52 CAL.3D 1. AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY) CORPORATIONS GENERALLY OWE NO FIDUCIARY DUTIES TO CREDITORS. DELAWARE LAW APPLIES TO THIS AND THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENT SUPERVISION) SEE STATE FARM V. SUPERIOR COURT (2003) 114 CAL.APP.4TH 434. DELAWARE LAW SHIELDS DIRECTORS FROM LIABILITY FOR A BREACH OF THE DUTY OF CARE UNLESS IT IS ALSO A BREACH OF THE DUTY OF LOYALTY UNDER SECTION 102(b)(7) OF THE DELAWARE GENERAL CORPORATE LAW. THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION (UCL) DOES NOT ALLEGE THE DEFENDANTS' PERSONAL PARTICIPATION IN THE UNLAWFUL PRACTICES. =(302/CWW)
